title: Reserialize fails fast when the bridge is offline

steps.title: Prerequisites
steps.body: Bridge running initially (so the offline step is attributable). Uses the standard manual offline pattern (engine-profiles/unity.md → Bridge-offline scenario pattern). The app has no HTTP route to stop/start the bridge in v1, so stop/start are operator-driven via the Unity toolbar, gated by `manual` actions and confirmed by `bridge_status`.

steps.title: Stage a fixture path (while the bridge is still up)
Stop the bridge via the Unity toolbar (manual gate)

actions.note: In the Unity Editor, click the bridge toolbar toggle to STOP the bridge (or close the Editor). Do not proceed until the toolbar shows the bridge as stopped.

steps.title: Run reserialize in the MCP client (bridge down)
Expected outcomes
items: The bridge_status mcp_tool in the go-offline step reports the bridge as stopped/dead before you continue
reserialize returns a live-unavailable error (reserialize is not a batch tool, so there is no Node-side fallback)
error does not hang; the agent can recover by reconnecting the Editor
mutation.success is false / absent (no mutation happened while offline)

steps.title: Paste the agent output (raw error envelope)

actions.note: In the Unity Editor, click the bridge toolbar toggle to START the bridge. Wait for it to reconnect before continuing.

reset.note: Reset order is reverse by step: the fixture delete runs, and the go-offline reset restarts the bridge and confirms readiness via bridge_status. Prefer the CLI `unity-open-mcp wait-for-ready` for a stronger readiness check if bridge_status alone is ambiguous. The bridge MUST be back online before the next scenario runs.
